value_definitions.name: dry steam
value_definitions.definition: Projects that use steam directly from a geothermal reservoir to turn a turbine.

value_definitions.name: flash steam - single
value_definitions.definition: Projects where the high-pressure geothermal fluid is converted to steam only once.

value_definitions.name: flash steam - double
value_definitions.definition: Projects where the remaining water after the first flash is still warm enough to flash again and used to run a second, low-pressure steam turbine.

value_definitions.name: flash steam - triple
value_definitions.definition: Such systems use three flashes to generate three different steam pressures: high, intermediate, and low. The steam flows are then used to rotate two or more turbines.

value_definitions.name: flash steam - unknown
value_definitions.definition: Projects where the high-pressure geothermal fluid is converted to steam but the number of flashes is unknown.

value_definitions.name: binary cycle
value_definitions.definition: Projects that transfer the heat from geothermal fluid to another liquid. The heat causes the second liquid to turn to steam, which is used to drive a turbine.

value_definitions.name: EGS (Enhanced Geothermal Systems)
value_definitions.definition: Projects that creates machine-made geothermal reservoirs in regions where there is naturally hot rock but insufficient permeability of fluid for a traditional geothermal power plant.

value_definitions.name: AGS (Advanced Geothermal Systems)
value_definitions.definition: Projects that use deep, large, artificial closed-loop circuits in which a working fluid is circulated and heated by sub-surface rocks through conductive heat transfer.

value_definitions.name: GGS (Geopressured Geothermal Systems)
value_definitions.definition: Pojects that repurpose fracking technology to extract thermal energy from miles below the Earth’s surface.

value_definitions.name: unknown type
value_definitions.definition: The type of geothermal power is not specified.

fields.definition_from_readme: See rows 16-23 above.

value_definitions.name: operating
value_definitions.definition: Commercial operation date achieved.

value_definitions.name: construction
value_definitions.definition: Equipment installation has begun (not just clearing/roads).

value_definitions.name: pre-construction
value_definitions.definition: Projects that are actively moving forward in seeking governmental approvals, land rights, or financing.

value_definitions.name: announced
value_definitions.definition: Projects that been publicly reported but have not yet moved actively forward by applying for permits or seeking land, material, or financing. Examples: (1) projects are the potential “Unit 2” at a location where “Unit 1” is currently under development, (2) Projects that are described in long-range company or governmental planning documents.

value_definitions.name: shelved
value_definitions.definition: Suspension of the project operation has been announced.

value_definitions.name: shelved - inferred 2 y
value_definitions.definition: The project is inferred to be shelved because, or no progress has been observed for at least two years.

value_definitions.name: mothballed
value_definitions.definition: Disused but not dismantled.

value_definitions.name: retired
value_definitions.definition: Dismantled.

value_definitions.name: cancelled
value_definitions.definition: A cancellation announcement has been made.

value_definitions.name: cancelled - inferred 4 y
value_definitions.definition: The project is inferred to be cancelled because, or no progress has been observed for at least four years

fields.definition_from_readme: The company that operates the project.
fields.notes_from_readme: The company that performs day-to-day operations and maintenance of the project.
